mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a reduction in supply-side pressures that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when combined with a decline in the unemployment rate.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the GDP deflator signals a reduction in supply-side pressures that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, especially when combined with a decline in the federal funds rate.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, especially when combined with a decline in initial unemployment claims.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the CPI signals a reduction in inflationary pressures that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when combined with a decline in the federal funds rate.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
